tasks.promptTemplate: Extract the service name, environment, owner, and enabled feature flags from the supplied application configuration snippet. Return only the declared structured object.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: Applications and agents routinely ask models for machine-readable extraction from small code or config snippets.

tasks.promptTemplate: Review the short deployment note and call record_plan with the component, risk level, and two recommended follow-up checks.

function.name: record_plan
function.description: Record a concise operational review plan.

tasks.normalUsageRationale: Coding and operations agents use tool/function calls to return structured plans and findings without side effects.

tasks.promptTemplate: Write a careful review of the supplied module summary. Cover responsibilities, likely edge cases, observability gaps, and a short implementation plan.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: Interactive coding tools stream longer reviews, plans, and explanations during ordinary development workflows.

tasks.promptTemplate: Use the shared project context prefix and answer the current implementation question in a concise engineering style.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: IDE agents repeatedly send stable project context with different ordinary questions, which can open cache usage and charge reconciliation evidence.

tasks.promptTemplate: Given the supplied deterministic changelog, list exactly the three deployment checks in priority order.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: Users often rerun the same stable instruction during a session to confirm consistent model behavior.

tasks.promptTemplate: Answer from the supplied authoritative release record: which release train owns invoice reconciliation?
tasks.normalUsageRationale: RAG, support, and test agents answer ordinary questions against supplied authoritative context.

tasks.promptTemplate: Summarize the supplied retry-safe operation note and include the operation name, expected outcome, and one verification step.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: Production clients attach logical operation IDs and rely on provider SDK defaults while making ordinary requests.

tasks.promptTemplate: Independently summarize one assigned repository maintenance note and return two concrete next actions.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: Developers and CI systems often run a bounded wave of independent normal model tasks concurrently.

tasks.promptTemplate: Summarize the supplied maintenance ticket in one paragraph and list the owner and due date.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: An ordinary Anthropic Messages request opens provider-specific token recount or gross-bound fallback evidence when Anthropic is selected.

tasks.promptTemplate: Create a structured implementation checkpoint from the supplied issue note with title, status, and next action.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: Applications use OpenAI Responses for structured generation in normal workflow automation.

tasks.promptTemplate: Summarize the supplied engineering note in three sentences with the main decision and follow-up owner.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: Every successful or failed provider call through the proxy carries timing, usage, and pricing status evidence.

tasks.promptTemplate: Summarize the supplied neutral engineering change log in two concise bullet points.
tasks.normalUsageRationale: Normal traffic can be passively inspected for governance and provider moderation evidence without manufacturing a signal.

surfaces.label: JSON/schema structured output
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Structured output extraction is normal application behavior.

surfaces.label: Registered output schema contract
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Registered schemas are ordinary contracts for machine-readable model output.

surfaces.label: Tool schema call validity
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Tool/function calls are a normal interface for coding and operations agents.

surfaces.label: Streaming terminal evidence
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Interactive agents normally stream long reviews and explanations.

surfaces.label: Cache usage, discount at risk, and charge reconciliation
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Repeated project context can produce cache usage in ordinary IDE-agent traffic.

surfaces.label: Single-window repeated prompt drift
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Users rerun stable prompts to confirm behavior during a session.

surfaces.label: Known-answer contract
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: RAG and support workflows answer against supplied authoritative records.

surfaces.label: Request ID uniqueness
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Real clients attach unique logical operation identifiers.

surfaces.label: SDK-native retry evidence
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Provider SDK retry paths and bounded concurrency occur in normal production and CI flows.

surfaces.label: Anthropic token cross-check
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Ordinary Anthropic Messages traffic opens Anthropic-specific token cross-checking.

surfaces.label: OpenAI Responses structured route
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: OpenAI Responses structured calls are normal application traffic.

surfaces.measure: Latency, token, usage, and pricing status
surfaces.label: Automatic latency and token accounting
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Every real provider call carries timing, usage, and pricing status evidence.

surfaces.label: Organic security and governance overlay
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Normal traffic is passively inspected for governance evidence.

surfaces.label: Organic provider moderation overlay
surfaces.normalUsageRationale: Normal OpenAI traffic can carry provider moderation evidence organically.
